summary: Replaced several raw numeric item-kind comparisons against itGetKind with named item kind constants, and cleaned up ft_0877 predicate return types from s32 to bool where the implementations return true/false-style values. The PR was small and mechanical but improved item-kind readability across fighter, player bonus, and stage code.
what_changed: Changed itGetKind comparisons in fighter.c from raw constants 0x67 and 0x1C to It_Kind_Peach_Parasol and It_Kind_Hammer.
Updated matching declarations in ft_0877.h so the header reflects the bool return types used by the corresponding implementations.
Replaced raw item constants in ft_0877.c: 0x1E became It_Kind_ScBall and 0x7 became It_Kind_Dosei.
Changed grcastle.c item-kind comparison from 0xA0 to Pokemon_Random in grCastle_801D06CC_sub.
Changed plbonuslib.c item-kind comparisons from 7 and 6 to It_Kind_Dosei and It_Kind_BombHei.
smart_moves: Used named enum-style constants at itGetKind call sites, making item comparisons self-documenting without altering control flow.
Kept the cleanup narrow: only call sites with clear item-kind equivalents were changed, minimizing risk.
Synchronized implementation and header return types for ft_0877 helpers in the same PR, avoiding prototype drift.
Converted only predicate-like ft_0877 functions to bool while leaving non-boolean accessors such as ft_80087A80, ft_80087A8C, ft_80087A98, ft_80087AB4, ft_80087B34, and void setters unchanged.
decomp_lessons: When a function such as itGetKind returns a domain-specific kind value, prefer named It_Kind or related constants over hex or decimal literals.
Predicate helpers that return TEST expressions, explicit true/false, or single-bit flag checks can often be typed as bool, but headers must be updated together with source definitions.
Semantic enum replacements are useful decomp cleanup because they preserve generated code intent while making future symbol discovery and review easier.
Do not globally convert all s32-returning helpers to bool; preserve integer, float, byte, and void accessors where the return value is not clearly boolean.
Item-kind constants may appear in multiple subsystems, including fighter state handling, player bonus accounting, and stage logic, so cleanup searches should not be limited to item source files.
naming_conventions: Named item constants use the It_Kind_* form, as in It_Kind_Peach_Parasol, It_Kind_Hammer, It_Kind_ScBall, It_Kind_Dosei, and It_Kind_BombHei.
The random Pokemon item/stage-related constant appears as Pokemon_Random rather than an It_Kind_* spelling in the shown grcastle.c replacement.
Boolean predicate helpers in ft_0877 retained their existing address-derived names but now expose bool return types.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Return-type cleanup from s32 to bool was applied to functions already returning true/false or bit-test predicate results, which is typically a low-risk type improvement if codegen remains stable.
Named constant substitution at equality and inequality comparisons against itGetKind is a semantic readability change that should not alter emitted comparison values when the constants are correct.
The TEST macro remained in place; the PR did not attempt to refactor predicate expression structure beyond return type cleanup.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only states: "Also clean up some ft_0877 return types"; no further rationale was available.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining itGetKind comparisons against raw decimal or hex literals.
Audit other ft_0877 helpers or nearby fighter helper files for s32 return types that are actually predicates.
Verify whether Pokemon_Random is intentionally separate from It_Kind_* naming or whether additional item-kind aliases exist for Pokemon-related values.
Look for other headers where source return types and prototypes may disagree after predicate cleanup.
